approach: Structural CR distilled from cyberuni/cyberplace#360 and operator-directed: split the worktree capability node into two SURFACE nodes because the CLI and the library API genuinely diverge (the availability predicate is injectable on the library seam but a CLI verb can only use the default gate). cli/worktree (NEW surface node) owns invocation + presentation — the cyber-mux worktree verbs, flag defaults, human-table rendering, exit codes — plus a NEW `worktree provision` verb; mux/worktree is re-scoped to the surface-independent library seam (provisionWorktree/WorktreeApi incl. the API-only injectable predicate, git-owns-facts, removal-never-delegated, gate ordering, disposability determination). Root spec.md placement map gains a bounded surface-axis exception to capability-first.

Blast radius MEDIUM: 5 spec files + a new node — cli/README.md, cli/worktree/README.md, cli/worktree/worktree.feature (new), mux/worktree/worktree.feature + README.md (re-scoped), spec.md (placement map). Source adds one CLI verb (worktreeProvisionCommand over provisionWorktree with the default gate) in a following deliver step — a new, additive feature. Package is 0.0.0 (Compatibility inert at this gate; a changeset lands with the source).

Edit class is a re-open of a frozen file: classify-edit-class reports mux/worktree.feature MIXED (5 added / 4 modified / 28 removed) — the 28 'removed' are RELOCATED to cli/worktree, not deleted; coverage conservation was verified scenario-by-scenario by the cold spec-judge (37 baseline -> 42 union, +5 new provision-verb scenarios, later +3 remediation scenarios). The re-open is ratified by the mission mandate (operator explicitly directed the full CLI/API surface split; #360 is the durable finding). Clearance is pre-authorized in the CR and its substantive bar (no acceptance weakened) is met by relocation-with-conservation. Leash auto-spec: high novelty (first use of the surface-axis exception, a structural precedent) lands provisionally in the async review queue rather than a hard stop; the cold judge is ALIGNED and coverage is conserved, so self-assert within the spec leash and let a human ratify or kick back.

why.floor: Clearance fires and CLEARS. classify-edit-class reports mux/worktree.feature MIXED (5 added / 4 modified / 28 removed) — a genuine re-open, ratified by the mission mandate (operator-directed full split; cyberuni/cyberplace#360). The 28 removed scenarios are RELOCATED to the new cli/worktree.feature, not deleted: the cold spec-judge verified coverage conservation scenario-by-scenario, and every baseline When/Then survives in exactly one of the two suites (the one dropped CLI-marker-suppression assertion was caught and restored as an additive CLI scenario before approval). cli/worktree.feature is a brand-new @frozen suite (all-added). No Conflict — no shared-When contradiction between the two suites. Compatibility inert (package 0.0.0; the new CLI verb's source + changeset land in the deliver step).
why.blast: medium — a structural split across 5 spec files plus a new surface node (cli/), and a placement-map change adding the surface-axis exception. No source change in THIS gate; the worktree provision verb source is the next step.
why.novelty: high — first use of the surface-axis exception to capability-first: a public surface that genuinely diverges from its capability earns its own node, the shared contract staying in the capability. A structural precedent, which is why it lands provisionally for async ratification.
why.confidence: high — cold spec-judge at depth 1, re-deriving its own oracle, returned ALIGNED with oracle/builder/architect all PASS after one remediation round. It PASSED the architect smear-vs-divergence question on evidence (zero duplicate assertions across the 42-scenario union; the injectable predicate is a callable a CLI flag cannot express; cli/ holds exactly one node and admission is tied to genuine divergence). Two blocking findings were caught and closed additively: a dropped undeterminable-signal marker-suppression assertion (restored as a paired guard+positive CLI scenario) and an unbacked prune Use Case (backed with a bare/--force scenario pair). Two prior rounds were governance pre-flight rejections (producer under-declared oracle/builder then architect bars) — refiled and passed. Mechanical checks green: check:features 8/8, check-suite OK, check-spec-state OK, cli scenario map 1:1 (31/31). Self-asserted within the auto-spec leash; lands provisionally in the async review queue: ratify or kick back.

why.floor: none at the impl gate. The only new implementation is the worktree provision CLI verb (worktreeProvisionCommand in cli.ts), wired over the already-shipped provisionWorktree seam with the DEFAULT gate and no predicate injection — the surface divergence. Relocated CLI scenarios and re-scoped seam scenarios changed spec TEXT only; their behavior and pre-existing verifications are unchanged, impl-sync held by the full suite staying green.
why.blast: small — one new CLI verb delegating straight to the seam; no existing behavior touched.
why.novelty: moderate — a new shipped CLI verb (cyber-mux worktree provision), additive.
why.confidence: high — cold impl-judge at depth 1 re-deriving each oracle independently returned IMPLEMENTATION_PASS: all 5 frozen provision-verb scenarios carry a real, falsifiable, passing verification (reuse-and-report-reclaim / create-at-sibling / --base / --path / no-predicate-injection). Default-gate/no-injection divergence confirmed at source (provisionWorktree called with no available key -> isWorktreeRemovable). Clean structural read (CLI-altitude delegation, no seam logic duplicated, no absorption). Full suite 808/808 green post-relocation; typecheck clean. Self-asserted within the auto-spec leash.

note: Impl-judge observation (in-repo, cyber-mux): the scenario-bridge (verify-scenarios) reports 31/31 UNBOUND for cli/worktree/worktree.feature — this project's test titles do not follow the @id-slug / verbatim-name binding convention the bridge needs, so every impl-judge pass on a worktree node pays full by-hand re-derivation with no bridge leverage. Adopting the binding convention across cyber-mux's suites would cut future impl-gate cost. Not filed (in-repo housekeeping, no confirmed permission); surfaced to the operator.
